headline: Software Engineering · ML/AI · Agentic Systems · Tooling
paragraphs: I am interested in the Software Engineer -- ML Core role because it sits close to the kind of engineering work I want to keep building: reliable tooling, backend foundations, and model-adjacent systems where data, runtime behavior, and operational context need to stay aligned.

My current public work is AgenticCareerBoost, a repository-backed workflow system that combines static publishing, LaTeX reports, validation scripts, project evidence, and review gates. It is not a product pitch; it is a way to make engineering work inspectable through source files, generated artifacts, and small repeatable checks.

Before engineering I spent 15 years in banking and insurance operations. That background makes me attentive to traceability, failure cases, data quality, handoffs, and regulated processes. I am strongest in teams where software judgment, documentation, and domain constraints matter together.

For Assaia, I would bring a systems-minded junior profile: Java/Spring and SQL foundations, a Python and ML/AI learning path, technical documentation habits, and the patience to build support tooling around model and pipeline work without turning the role into AI hype.
closing: I would welcome the chance to discuss how this background could support Assaia's ML Core engineering work.
public_note: Public example adapted from a past application draft. It is kept as a buildable template example, not as an active application.
parser_summary: Dídac Llorens; Barcelona; UOC Software Engineering; ML/AI specialization; agentic workflow systems; backend foundations; Python learning path; Java; Spring Boot; SQL; LaTeX; GitHub Actions; technical documentation; banking and insurance operations; regulated-domain maturity.
